chunks.text: A police officer, that is, a cop
is an important profession that protects the order of society.
They patrol the streets around the clock and react to incidents and accidents.
Their work involves many areas.
Investigating a crime, enforcing traffic laws, consulting with residents
dealing with the need to rid the streets of suspicious persons.
There are dramatic scenes of tracking down a killer
but much of daily duty is steady, quiet work.
Writing documents, investigating the scene, communication with an editor.
The role of the facility that manages a prisoner is also important.
The aim is not only to punish criminals but also to rehabilitate them.
But the work of police has moments that frighten.
Armed criminals, gunshots, explosion.
They often bear a risk to their own lives.
Police function only with the trust of citizens.
If there is violence or corruption, their reputation is lost in an instant.
In America, excessive violence against Black people has continued
and the " Black Lives Matter " movement has spread.
This is not a rebel against the entire police but a voice demanding reform.
Japanese police are called affectionately " Omawari - san.
" The koban ( police box ) system is unique in the world
providing local community - based security.
Police officers too are human.
They make mistakes, and they have emotions.
While we expect much of them, we must not demand perfection.
Mutual understanding and cooperation is the foundation of a safe society.
